jar_title: Each jar has its own story
testresults_residue_description: A strictly low detection threshold (<0.002) test confirmed no traces in the honey. Our bees thrive in Mānuka forests in remote corners of New Zealand, untouched by environmental toxins.
faq_question_one: What does Creamy Caramel Guarantee mean?
faq_answer_one: Nature's raw Mānuka honey is known for its delightful variability, but our traditional harvest and creaming process ensures that every jar arrives at your doorstep with a lusciously creamy texture. We stand behind the quality of our super honey, so if it doesn't meet our creamy caramel standard, we'll happily replace it.
faq_question_two: What is MGO? What do the numbers mean?
faq_answer_two: Methylglyoxal (MGO) is the unique naturally occurring antibacterial properties in Mānuka honey. A higher MGO value indicates higher antibacterial activity, it also indicates higher levels of other active, beneficial compounds in the Mānuka honey. In simple terms, the higher the MGO, the more potent the honey is.
faq_question_three: What is the Art of Ethical Beekeeping?
faq_answer_three: Ethical Beekeeping is the heart of what we do at Manukora. It involves sustainable practices that ensure the health and happiness of our bees. We keep our hives in natural habitats with minimal transportation, do not feed our bees synthetic substitutes, and never use antibiotics or harmful chemicals. This approach not only supports a thriving bee population but also ensures the highest quality and purity of our Manuka honey. Learn more here or check out an interview with one of our master beekeepers here.
faq_question_four: Why is Mānuka honey different from my local honey?
faq_answer_four: While we wholeheartedly support the wonders of local honey and its array of benefits, Mānuka honey stands in a league of its own. Produced exclusively in the remote Manuka tea tree forests of New Zealand, it boasts exceptional levels of bioactive properties, such as MGO, Leptosperin, and oligosaccharides that are not found in other honey types.
faq_answer_five: Take 1 heaped teaspoon each morning. Enjoy Manukora straight or supercharge your morning beverage or breakfast. Due to the lower GI (slower release) of Mānuka honey, you can also use throughout the day. To get the most out of the bioactives in Mānuka, take daily on an empty stomach and avoid adding it to boiling water (hot is okay).
faq_answer_six: 1-3 heaped teaspoons daily. It is perfectly fine to eat more as you see fit, especially when using Mānuka as an alternative to refined sugar in your diet!
faq_answer_seven: Manukora Manuka honey features a rich caramel and toffee flavor, accented by light floral notes. Its smooth, creamy texture makes it perfect for enjoying on its own or as a sweetener in your favorite dishes and drinks.

additionalfacts_title: Raw Mānuka honey - the smartest carrier on Earth
additionalfacts_description: Mānuka honey acts as a natural carrier to enhance bioavailability. Its enzymes and sugars help transport nutrients more efficiently into the bloodstream. Unlike capsules or tablets, honey supports faster absorption by coating and delivering active compounds directly through mucous membranes, making it an ideal carrier for functional ingredients.
jar_title_ingredients: Each ingredient has its own story
testresults_mgomodal: MGO is a unique naturally-occurring chemical compound found in Manuka honey that gives it its unique antibacterial properties.
testresults_umfmodal: UMF stands for 'Unique Mānuka Factor', which reflects the concentration of 3 signature compounds found in genuine Mānuka honey, MGO, DHA and leptosperin. It is designed to provide absolute assurance of authenticity and potency.
testresults_glyphosatemodal: Glyphosate is a broad-spectrum systemic herbicide and crop desiccant.
